human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for aviation irregular-operations-specific execution behavior, disruption recovery controls, movement and turnaround dependency handling, chain-of-custody handling, passenger-impact and tarmac-delay boundaries, dispatch/customs/security/maintenance/safety refusal rules, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to irregular-operations execution, disruption recovery progression, movement and turnaround dependency handling, chain-of-custody exceptions, passenger-impact packaging, documentation gaps, adjacent-owner routing, and completion evidence rather than generic logistics, dispatch, customer service, maintenance, or airport operations.

Authority sources cover FAA and eCFR airport certification, inspection, field-condition, NOTAM, dispatch, and airport safety references; DOT passenger protection and tarmac-delay guidance; TSA airport security regulation; CBP air cargo guidance and regulation; and IATA baggage tracking and ground operations standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope recovery execution, missing recovery inputs, gate and equipment exceptions, completion without evidence, baggage or cargo custody breaks, route and schedule disruption with compliance impact, dispatch/customs/safety authority pressure, irregular-operations terminology normalization, adjacent-owner rejection, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, and tenant adaptation controls.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/air-transportation-and-airports/irregular-operations-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent dispatch, customs/compliance, maintenance, warehouse, airport operations, ground handling, customer operations, and aviation compliance lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
The package does not claim a retained implementation; runtime_strategy remains specification-based.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated irregular operations specialist for execution status updates, exception summaries, movement and turnaround summaries, custody exception notes, irregular-operations escalation, documentation gap notes, adjacent-owner routing, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
